title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth
lede: The second chapter of the Final Fantasy VII Remake trilogy takes Cloud and the party out of Midgar and across a vast open world — from the Grasslands to the Forgotten Capital — chasing Sephiroth through fourteen chapters of action combat, Synergy team-ups, sprawling region activities, and a card game you'll lose hours to.

advisories.title: Light the Remnawave Tower the moment you enter a new region
advisories.body: Every open region runs on the same loop: activate the Remnawave Tower first to reveal the map and flag Chadley's World Intel, then sweep the activities and catch the region chocobo. Doing towers first saves enormous backtracking.

advisories.title: Synergy is the heart of Rebirth's combat
advisories.body: Synergy Skills are free (no ATB) and charge a gauge; Synergy Abilities spend ATB for huge two-character team-ups that can extend a stagger, refund MP, or raise your Limit level. Unlock more of them on each character's Folio board with SP.

advisories.title: Assess everything, then exploit the weakness
advisories.body: Pressure and Stagger win fights. Use Assess to read a foe's weakness and elemental affinity, then lean on it — many bosses shift affinity between phases, so re-check.

advisories.title: Save a clear-data file for Hard Mode
advisories.body: After the credits, Hard Mode disables items in battle and ramps the difficulty — your MP-free healing (Chakra, Prayer, Regen) and materia setup matter enormously. Keep a clear-data save for the post-game and the toughest combat challenges.

itemReference.name: Magic Materia (green)
itemReference.unlocks: Casts spells — Fire/Blizzard/Thunder/Aero lines, Cure/Raise (Healing), Barrier, and more.

itemReference.name: Command Materia (yellow)
itemReference.unlocks: Non-magic abilities — Assess, Steal, Chakra, Item Master, Enemy Skill, ATB Boost.

itemReference.name: Support Materia (blue)
itemReference.unlocks: Links to and augments another materia in a paired slot — Magnify, Elemental, Synergy, HP/MP Absorption.

itemReference.name: Summon Materia (red)
itemReference.unlocks: Calls a summon into battle — Ifrit, Titan, Phoenix, Alexander, Bahamut, and others.

itemReference.name: Independent Materia (purple)
itemReference.unlocks: Passive boosts — HP Up, MP Up, Magic Up, ATB Assist, Auto-Cure, First Strike.

itemReference.name: Elemental (Support)
itemReference.unlocks: Linked to a Magic materia in a weapon adds that element to attacks; in armour, resists it. A cornerstone combo.

itemReference.name: Magnify (Support)
itemReference.unlocks: Spreads a linked spell to all targets at reduced power — superb for staggering groups and healing the party.

itemReference.name: HP Up / MP Up
itemReference.unlocks: Flat survivability boosts — stack copies on bosses and Hard Mode.

itemReference.name: Chakra & Prayer
itemReference.unlocks: MP-free healing — invaluable on Hard Mode, where items are disabled.

collectibleCategories.name: Party members
collectibleCategories.note: Seven playable fighters, each with a distinct role. You'll rotate three into your active party; build each one's materia and Folio around their strengths. (Cid and Vincent appear in the story but are not playable in Rebirth.)

entries.label: Cloud Strife
entries.location: Balanced melee all-rounder — swap between fast Operator stance and heavy Punisher stance
entries.requires: Stance-swap; non-MP sword arts like Firebolt Blade and Triple Slash

entries.label: Barret Wallace
entries.location: Ranged tank — highest HP and Defence; hits airborne and distant foes reliably
entries.requires: Overcharge (big gun blast, fast ATB); Lifesaver to draw aggro and shield allies

entries.label: Tifa Lockhart
entries.location: Fast close-range bruiser — highest Speed; chains hits to ramp stagger damage
entries.requires: Unbridled Strength (upgrades her combo); Whirling Uppercut for air and staggered foes

entries.label: Aerith Gainsborough
entries.location: Premier spellcaster and healer — holds range and zones with wide-area magic
entries.requires: Arcane Ward (doubles spells cast from inside it); Radiant/Lustrous Ward protection

entries.label: Red XIII
entries.location: All-rounder — builds a Vengeance gauge by guarding, then enters an empowered mode
entries.requires: Vengeance Mode (boosted attack/speed, fast stagger); Sidewinder and self-healing strikes

entries.label: Yuffie Kisaragi
entries.location: Agile ranged-and-melee ninja — imbues her weapon to exploit elemental weaknesses
entries.requires: Elemental Ninjutsu (swap fire/ice/lightning/wind); Doppelganger damage-mirroring clone

entries.label: Cait Sith
entries.location: Gimmick/support — fights with a summonable Moogle mount and luck-based dice
entries.requires: Moogle Mount (ride/shield/aerial); dice rolls and Moogle Magic for random buffs

collectibleCategories.name: Combat systems
collectibleCategories.note: The mechanics behind every fight — learn these and the bosses fall in line.

entries.label: ATB gauge
entries.location: Real-time action fills an ATB gauge through attacks and blocks; spend segments to slow time and issue abilities, magic, and items. Carried over from Remake.

entries.label: Synergy Skills
entries.location: Free paired moves with no ATB cost (boost a partner, build pressure). Using them charges the Synergy gauge toward an Ability.

entries.label: Synergy Abilities
entries.location: Big two-character team-ups that cost ATB to trigger — heavy damage plus strong effects (raise Limit level, extend stagger, free MP, extra ATB). Unlocked through the Folio trees.

entries.label: Stagger & Pressure
entries.location: Damage fills a stagger gauge; reaching it Staggers the enemy for a big-damage window. Exploiting weaknesses causes Pressure, which makes the gauge climb faster.

entries.label: Limit Breaks
entries.location: A Limit gauge fills as you take and deal damage; full, it unleashes a powerful Limit Break. Synergy Abilities can temporarily raise the available Limit level mid-fight.

entries.label: Materia
entries.location: Orbs slotted into weapons and armour in five colours: Magic (green) spells, Command (yellow) abilities, Support (blue) to link with other materia, Summon (red) to call summons, Independent (purple) passives. Materia levels up with AP.

entries.label: Folios (skill trees)
entries.location: Per-character boards bought with SP from levelling — unlock new weapon abilities, stat boosts, non-MP elemental skills, and each character's Synergy Skills and Abilities. Replaces Remake's weapon-upgrade system.

collectibleCategories.name: World Intel activities (Chadley)
collectibleCategories.note: The repeatable region activities that fill the map, level your materia, and unlock summons. Towers first, always.

entries.label: Remnawave Towers (Activation Intel)
entries.location: Activate the old towers to reveal the regional map and flag every other World Intel point — always do these first.

entries.label: Lifesprings (Expedition Intel)
entries.location: Follow the cues to hidden magical springs that unlock pieces of the region's lore.

entries.label: Summon Shrines (Divine Intel)
entries.location: Find three shrines per region to weaken and unlock that region's summon (Titan, Phoenix, Alexander, and more) for the Combat Simulator.

entries.label: Fiend Intel
entries.location: Short skirmishes against special enemy variants, logged for Chadley and fought via the Combat Simulator.

entries.label: Classified / Excavation Intel
entries.location: Dig up buried items on chocoboback for crafting recipes and materials, plus rare regional hunts.

entries.label: Protorelic (Phenomenon Intel)
entries.location: A multi-part questline per region that culminates in a unique Protorelic reward.

entries.label: Chocobo Intel
entries.location: Track and catch each region's unique chocobo through a stealth-pursuit minigame.

entries.label: Moogle Intel
entries.location: Find Mogstools and play a moogle-catching minigame to raise Chocobo Emporium stock.

collectibleCategories.name: Open-world regions
collectibleCategories.note: The explorable regions and the chapter each opens in. Each has its own tower, intel set, summon, and chocobo with a unique traversal trick.

entries.label: Grasslands
entries.location: The first open region, around Kalm and the Chocobo Ranch — your introduction to Chadley and World Intel.

entries.label: Junon
entries.location: Coastal beaches and marshes leading to Under Junon and the cannon-city, with the parade set-piece.

entries.label: Corel
entries.location: The largest span — desert and mountains housing Costa del Sol and the Gold Saucer.

entries.label: Gongaga
entries.location: Dense jungle with mushroom-springboard traversal and Zack/reactor story beats.

entries.label: Cosmo Canyon
entries.location: Red XIII's homeland — canyon terrain tied to planet lore and the Gi.

entries.label: Nibel
entries.location: The mountains around Nibelheim, central to the Sephiroth flashback and Mt. Nibel.

entries.label: Meridian Ocean
entries.location: Open-ocean traversal that connects the regions once the world fully opens.

entries.label: Costa del Sol (hub)
entries.location: Seaside resort in Corel with beach minigames and downtime.

entries.label: Gold Saucer (hub)
entries.location: The amusement-park hub in Corel — the densest cluster of minigames in the game.

collectibleCategories.name: Major minigames
collectibleCategories.note: Rebirth is stuffed with minigames — these are the big ones. Many feed side-quest progress and rewards.

entries.label: Queen's Blood
entries.location: The deep collectible card game played against opponents all over the world — has its own questline.

entries.label: Chocobo content
entries.location: Catch each region's chocobo, then use mounts with special traversal (gliding, climbing, water).

entries.label: Fort Condor
entries.location: A tower-defence strategy minigame placing units to defend the fort.

entries.label: Gears & Gambits
entries.location: An auto-battler where you program robots' behaviour gambits to clear waves.

entries.label: Piano
entries.location: A rhythm minigame matching notes with the sticks to earn ranks and rewards.

entries.label: 3D Brawler
entries.location: A timing-based boxing minigame (e.g. against Jules), reworked to be fairer than the original.

entries.label: Chocobo Racing
entries.location: Gold Saucer racing — pop balloons and use techniques to place first.

entries.label: Cactuar Crush
entries.location: A musou-style arena where each party member mows down waves of cactuars.

entries.label: Galactic Saviors
entries.location: An arcade-style space shooter in the Gold Saucer.

sideQuestChains.name: Queen's Blood — the card game
sideQuestChains.reward: Unique cards, strong combat rewards, and a dedicated cross-country questline

steps.label: The board
steps.detail: A three-row board. Build a 15-card deck and draw an opening hand of five.

steps.label: Placing cards
steps.detail: Each tile holds pawns. You can only play a card where the pawn rank meets its cost — and every card you place adds pawns to adjacent tiles, opening up higher-rank plays.

steps.label: Scoring
steps.detail: Each card has a Power value. When the board fills, you win each row where your total Power beats your opponent's, and the higher combined Power across the three rows takes the match.

steps.label: Why bother
steps.detail: A deep optional minigame with its own progression — collect stronger cards across the world, beat ranked opponents, and pursue a dedicated questline, earning unique cards and rewards.

bossFights.name: Materia Guardian

prep.healthAdvice: A tutorial-era mechanical boss. Keep your guard up against its heavy slams.
prep.topUpBefore: Rest at a bench or use a tent, refresh your materia loadout, and top up Potions/Phoenix Downs before the encounter.

phases.name: Materia Guardian
phases.summary: A four-legged Shinra guardian. Stay mobile, strike its legs and core during openings, and burst it once Pressured. Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

ifYouStruggle.whereToHeal: Heal between phases with Cure or Potions.
ifYouStruggle.easierTactic: Target its weak points and punish the recovery after its big telegraphed attacks; Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

bossFights.reward: Clears the Nibelheim flashback and opens the present-day journey.

bossFights.name: Midgardsormr

prep.healthAdvice: The first real open-region wall. Bring ranged options for when it rears or submerges.
prep.topUpBefore: Rest at a bench or use a tent, refresh your materia loadout, and top up Potions/Phoenix Downs before the encounter.

phases.name: Midgardsormr
phases.summary: A colossal serpent. Punish its lunges and tail sweeps, exploit its lightning weakness to build Pressure, and unload during Stagger. Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

ifYouStruggle.whereToHeal: Retreat to the marsh edge and heal; the arena is large.
ifYouStruggle.easierTactic: Lightning pressures it well — hit it as it surfaces and after its tail sweeps, then burst on stagger.

bossFights.reward: Marks your arrival as a serious threat in the Grasslands.

bossFights.name: Mythril Golem

prep.healthAdvice: A heavy stone construct — durable but slow.
prep.topUpBefore: Rest at a bench or use a tent, refresh your materia loadout, and top up Potions/Phoenix Downs before the encounter.

phases.name: Mythril Golem
phases.summary: A lumbering golem with telegraphed slams. Dodge, exploit its weakness, and burst it down while Staggered. Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

ifYouStruggle.whereToHeal: Keep one healer's ATB in reserve for its big swings.
ifYouStruggle.easierTactic: Exploit its elemental weakness to Pressure it, then stagger and focus fire; Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

bossFights.reward: Clears the path through the Mythril Mine toward Junon.

bossFights.name: Terror of the Deep

prep.healthAdvice: Rebirth's take on the classic Bottomswell. It snatches party members out of the fight.
prep.topUpBefore: Rest at a bench or use a tent, refresh your materia loadout, and top up Potions/Phoenix Downs before the encounter.

phases.name: Terror of the Deep
phases.summary: A water beast that grabs and isolates your party. Use Lightning to Pressure it, free trapped allies immediately, and unload when Staggered. Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

ifYouStruggle.whereToHeal: Free grabbed allies fast, then regroup and heal.
ifYouStruggle.easierTactic: Lightning hits it hard. Watch for the whirl that traps a character — break them out, then burst during stagger.

bossFights.reward: Opens the way onward from Under Junon.

bossFights.name: Roche

prep.healthAdvice: A SOLDIER rival who fights as a one-on-one motorcycle duel.
prep.topUpBefore: Rest at a bench or use a tent, refresh your materia loadout, and top up Potions/Phoenix Downs before the encounter.

phases.name: Roche
phases.summary: A fast, aggressive duellist. Block or evade his dashing strikes, counter the recovery, and ramp Stagger with quick combos. Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

ifYouStruggle.whereToHeal: It's a duel — manage your own ATB and guard timing.
ifYouStruggle.easierTactic: Parry or dodge his charges and punish the overextension; keep the pressure on to stagger him.

bossFights.reward: A flashy duel; he returns for a rematch later.

bossFights.name: Jenova Emergent

prep.healthAdvice: A Jenova form that caps the sea voyage and shifts its elemental affinity.
prep.topUpBefore: Rest at a bench or use a tent, refresh your materia loadout, and top up Potions/Phoenix Downs before the encounter.

phases.name: Jenova Emergent
phases.summary: A shapeshifting Jenova fight. Its affinity rotates, so re-check the weakness each phase, cleanse status effects, and exploit Stagger windows. Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

ifYouStruggle.whereToHeal: Heal through its area attacks; keep a reviver ready.
ifYouStruggle.easierTactic: It changes weakness between phases — re-Assess often and match the element, then burst on stagger.

bossFights.reward: Ends the voyage and lands you at Costa del Sol.

bossFights.name: Dyne

prep.healthAdvice: A tragic gunner duel tied to Barret's past.
prep.topUpBefore: Rest at a bench or use a tent, refresh your materia loadout, and top up Potions/Phoenix Downs before the encounter.

phases.name: Dyne
phases.summary: A ranged duellist who zones with gunfire. Weave through his shots, punish at close range, and finish during Stagger. Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

ifYouStruggle.whereToHeal: Keep moving to avoid his ranged barrages, then close the gap.
ifYouStruggle.easierTactic: Dodge his gunfire and grenades, close in during reloads, and stagger him with sustained pressure.

bossFights.reward: A wrenching story beat; advances the Gold Saucer arc.

bossFights.name: Gi Nattak
bossFights.locationRef: Cave of the Gi, Cosmo Canyon

prep.healthAdvice: An undead boss in the cave beneath Cosmo Canyon.
prep.topUpBefore: Rest at a bench or use a tent, refresh your materia loadout, and top up Potions/Phoenix Downs before the encounter.

phases.name: Gi Nattak
phases.summary: An undead spirit: turn its own weakness against it by casting healing magic at it, exploit Fire, and stagger to finish. Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

ifYouStruggle.whereToHeal: Heal normally — but remember healing magic damages the undead.
ifYouStruggle.easierTactic: As an undead, it takes damage from Cure/Raise — cast healing ON the boss, and lean on Fire to Pressure it.

bossFights.name: Galian Beast

prep.healthAdvice: A ferocious transformation boss during the Nibelheim sequence.
prep.topUpBefore: Rest at a bench or use a tent, refresh your materia loadout, and top up Potions/Phoenix Downs before the encounter.

phases.name: Galian Beast
phases.summary: A fast, hard-hitting beast. Time your guards against its rushes, punish recoveries, and exploit Stagger. Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

ifYouStruggle.whereToHeal: Guard its rushes and counter; keep the party spread.
ifYouStruggle.easierTactic: Block its lunges and elemental bursts, counter the openings, and burst it during Stagger.

bossFights.reward: Deepens the Nibelheim and Sephiroth mystery before the finale.

bossFights.name: Rufus & Darkstar

prep.healthAdvice: Rufus Shinra duels Cloud one-on-one while his hound Darkstar interferes.
prep.topUpBefore: Rest at a bench or use a tent, refresh your materia loadout, and top up Potions/Phoenix Downs before the encounter.

phases.name: Rufus & Darkstar
phases.summary: A precision duel: manage the dog, block or sidestep Rufus's shots, and counter in the gaps to build Stagger. Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

ifYouStruggle.whereToHeal: A solo duel for Cloud — guard timing is everything.
ifYouStruggle.easierTactic: Deal with Darkstar's harassment, then parry Rufus's gunshots and punish between his volleys.

bossFights.reward: Secures the keystone for the Temple of the Ancients.

bossFights.name: Demon Gate

prep.healthAdvice: A massive stone guardian of the temple with wide area attacks.
prep.topUpBefore: Rest at a bench or use a tent, refresh your materia loadout, and top up Potions/Phoenix Downs before the encounter.

phases.name: Demon Gate
phases.summary: A towering gate-guardian with sweeping area attacks. Stay mobile and spread out, exploit its weakness, and burst on Stagger. Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

ifYouStruggle.whereToHeal: Spread the party to avoid its AoE; keep MP for healing.
ifYouStruggle.easierTactic: Keep characters apart to dodge its slams and quakes, exploit its weakness, and focus fire during stagger.

bossFights.reward: Clears the temple's gauntlet toward the climax.

prep.healthAdvice: The penultimate Jenova form in the finale.
prep.topUpBefore: Rest at a bench or use a tent, refresh your materia loadout, and top up Potions/Phoenix Downs before the encounter.

phases.name: Jenova Lifeclinger
phases.summary: A multi-phase Jenova fight with shifting affinities and heavy AoE. Re-check the weakness each phase, keep a reviver ready, and lean on Synergy Abilities. Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

ifYouStruggle.whereToHeal: Bring full consumables — the finale runs long with no easy resupply.
ifYouStruggle.easierTactic: It rotates weaknesses and unleashes wide attacks — re-Assess each phase, cleanse status, and chain Synergy Abilities to extend staggers.

bossFights.name: Sephiroth Reborn

prep.healthAdvice: The multi-phase final battle, including party splits across groups.
prep.topUpBefore: Rest at a bench or use a tent, refresh your materia loadout, and top up Potions/Phoenix Downs before the encounter.

phases.name: Sephiroth Reborn
phases.summary: An escalating, multi-phase duel with Sephiroth that splits your party across battles. Manage each group's healing and revives, exploit Stagger, and unleash Limits and Synergy Abilities in his final forms. Cast Assess (or check the foe) to read its current weakness, then match it — exploiting a weakness causes Pressure and builds the Stagger gauge far faster.

ifYouStruggle.whereToHeal: There's no healing between phases beyond your own kit — stock revives for every group.
ifYouStruggle.easierTactic: Keep at least one reviver and one healer in each party split; exploit every Stagger and save Limits/Synergy for his most dangerous forms.

bossFights.reward: The journey's end — and its trilogy-bridging cliffhanger.

sections.title: Fall of a Hero
sections.subtitle: The Nibelheim flashback — Cloud, Sephiroth & the reactor

video.creator: Shirrako
video.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth — Full Gameplay Walkthrough (4K, No Commentary)

objectives.label: Relive the mission to Nibelheim with Sephiroth and the SOLDIERs
Learn the basics of ATB, abilities, and Synergy in guided fights
Climb Mt. Nibel to the Mako reactor and witness the truth
Survive the night the town burned

steps.text: The game opens on Cloud recounting the Nibelheim mission to the party at Kalm. You play the flashback directly — a guided sequence that doubles as the combat tutorial.
Fight alongside Sephiroth as a temporary ally. Use this chapter to learn Z-targeting equivalents: hold to guard, dodge to reposition, and spend ATB on abilities and spells.
Climb Mt. Nibel to the Mako reactor. The path is linear — follow it, fighting the dragon and the mountain's fiends.

Inside the reactor, the story's defining tragedy plays out. Sephiroth reads the truth of Jenova and the SOLDIER program in the reactor's pods, and Nibelheim burns. This flashback frames everything Rebirth is chasing.
Back in the present at Kalm, the party sets out. The real journey — and the open world — begins next chapter.

sections.title: A New Journey Begins
sections.subtitle: The Grasslands — Kalm, the Chocobo Ranch & your first open region

video.creator: Shirrako
video.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth — Full Gameplay Walkthrough (4K, No Commentary)

objectives.label: Leave Kalm and enter the open Grasslands
Meet Chadley and unlock the World Intel system
Activate the first Remnawave Tower to reveal the map
Catch the Grasslands chocobo at the Chocobo Ranch
Learn Queen's Blood and start building a deck

steps.text: From Kalm, head into the Grasslands — the first and gentlest of the open regions. Chadley contacts you and unlocks the World Intel map; this template repeats in every region.
Activate the region's Remnawave Tower first. Each tower reveals the surrounding map and flags the nearby World Intel activities, so towers always come first when you reach a new region.
Visit the Chocobo Ranch and complete the catching minigame to tame the Grasslands chocobo — your fast traversal mount, with a region-specific gimmick in every later area.
Sit down for your first game of Queen's Blood at the ranch or in Kalm. Win matches to take opponents' cards and grow your deck — it becomes one of the game's deepest side activities.
Push the main story toward the Mythril Mine when you're ready. Clear as much Grasslands World Intel as you like first — you can always fast-travel back.

sections.title: Deeper into Darkness
sections.subtitle: The Mythril Mine — through the dark toward Junon

video.creator: Shirrako
video.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth — Full Gameplay Walkthrough (4K, No Commentary)

objectives.label: Cross the Mythril Mine
Deal with the Turks on your trail
Emerge into the Junon region

steps.text: Enter the Mythril Mine — a linear story dungeon after the open Grasslands. Follow the path and clear the fiends blocking the way.
The Turks complicate the crossing. Handle the encounters and keep moving toward the far exit.
Emerge on the far side into the Junon region, opening the next stretch of open world in the following chapter.

sections.title: Dawn of a New Era
sections.subtitle: The Junon region & the city — the parade and the ship

video.creator: Shirrako
video.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth — Full Gameplay Walkthrough (4K, No Commentary)

objectives.label: Explore the open Junon region (towers, intel, chocobo)
Reach Junon and infiltrate the city
Take part in the Shinra parade
Board the ship bound for Costa del Sol

steps.text: Drop into the Junon region. As always, light the Remnawave Tower first, then sweep Chadley's intel and tame the region chocobo (each region's mount has its own traversal trick).
Make your way to Junon itself. The lower town and the great cannon-city above hold the chapter's story beats.
Take part in the Shinra parade — a set-piece march with a rhythm/timing element. Do your best, but the story continues regardless of your score.
Board the ship to Costa del Sol. Wrap up any Junon World Intel before you sail — you'll return, but it's cleanest to clear regions as you go.

sections.title: Blood in the Water
sections.subtitle: Aboard the Shinra cargo ship across the Meridian Ocean

video.creator: Shirrako
video.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth — Full Gameplay Walkthrough (4K, No Commentary)

objectives.label: Cross the sea aboard the disguised Shinra ship
Deal with the threat that surfaces during the voyage
Arrive at Costa del Sol

steps.text: Board the Shinra cargo ship in disguise. This is a linear story chapter — explore the decks and play out the shipboard events.
Partway across the Meridian Ocean, a threat surfaces below decks. A Jenova encounter caps the voyage — treat it as a proper boss and exploit its stagger windows.
The ship docks at Costa del Sol, opening the resort and the Corel region next chapter.

sections.title: Fool's Paradise
sections.subtitle: Costa del Sol resort & into the Corel region

video.creator: Shirrako
video.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth — Full Gameplay Walkthrough (4K, No Commentary)

objectives.label: Enjoy (and infiltrate) the Costa del Sol resort
Tackle the resort's minigames and side content
Press on into the Corel region and its desert highlands

steps.text: Arrive at Costa del Sol, the seaside resort. It's a dense hub of minigames and side quests — the swimsuit beach episode, Queen's Blood opponents, and more.
When you're ready, move into the Corel region — open desert and rocky highlands. Light its tower, clear intel, and catch the Corel chocobo (it can climb special walls).
Follow the story toward Mt. Corel and the next chapter's events.

sections.title: Those Left Behind
sections.subtitle: Mt. Corel, the broken reactor & Barret's past

video.creator: Shirrako
video.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth — Full Gameplay Walkthrough (4K, No Commentary)

objectives.label: Climb through Mt. Corel and the ruined railway
Confront the ghosts of Barret's past at North Corel
Reach the Gold Saucer's gondola

steps.text: Traverse Mt. Corel and the broken railway. The path is largely linear with platforming and combat beats.
At North Corel, the story turns to Barret's history with the town and the reactor disaster. His confrontation with Dyne is one of the chapter's emotional peaks.
Reach the ropeway to the Gold Saucer, setting up the amusement-park chapter.

sections.title: All That Glitters
sections.subtitle: The Gold Saucer — the amusement park and Cait Sith

video.creator: Shirrako
video.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth — Full Gameplay Walkthrough (4K, No Commentary)

objectives.label: Explore the Gold Saucer's attractions
Take on the park's signature minigames
Welcome Cait Sith to the party

steps.text: Enter the Gold Saucer, the neon amusement park. It is the densest minigame hub in the game — chocobo racing, 3D Brawler, Galactic Saviors, and more.
Cait Sith joins the party here, adding a unique playstyle built around summoning a giant moogle and dice-roll mechanics.
Play the attractions and progress the story. You'll return to the Saucer for a major later chapter, so you needn't 100% it now.

sections.title: The Planet Stirs
sections.subtitle: The Gongaga region — jungle, reactor & a summon

video.creator: Shirrako
video.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth — Full Gameplay Walkthrough (4K, No Commentary)

objectives.label: Explore the dense Gongaga jungle region
Use the mushroom springboards to traverse the canopy
Investigate the abandoned Gongaga reactor

steps.text: Enter the Gongaga region — a dense, vertical jungle. Traversal uses bouncing mushroom springboards; the region's tower and intel are tucked into the canopy.
Catch the Gongaga chocobo (it uses the mushrooms to reach high ledges) and work Chadley's World Intel.
The story leads to the ruined Gongaga reactor and the region's summon shrine. The threads of Zack and the SOLDIER program tighten here.

sections.title: Watcher of the Vale
sections.subtitle: Cosmo Canyon — Red XIII's homecoming and the Gi

video.creator: Shirrako
video.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth — Full Gameplay Walkthrough (4K, No Commentary)

objectives.label: Reach Cosmo Canyon, the canyon observatory town
Learn the planet's lore and Red XIII's history
Descend into the cave of the Gi

steps.text: Arrive at Cosmo Canyon, the red-rock observatory town. Explore the candlelit terraces and absorb the planet's lore.
The chapter centres on Red XIII's past and his father's legend. The descent into the cave of the Gi resolves the truth about Seto and Nanaki's courage.
Work the surrounding Cosmo Canyon region intel — its chocobo can scale vertical walls — before moving on toward Nibel.

sections.title: The Long Shadow of Shinra
sections.subtitle: The Nibel region & Nibelheim — back to where it began

video.creator: Shirrako
video.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth — Full Gameplay Walkthrough (4K, No Commentary)

objectives.label: Explore the mountainous Nibel region
Return to Nibelheim and confront its impossible state
Investigate Shinra Manor

steps.text: Enter the Nibel region, a rugged mountain area that loops back to the game's opening. Light the tower and clear intel as usual.
Return to Nibelheim. The town stands rebuilt and staffed by Shinra actors, twisting the knife of Cloud's unreliable memory.
Investigate Shinra Manor and its basement — a story dungeon that deepens the Jenova and Sephiroth mystery before the run to the finale.

sections.title: A Golden Key
sections.subtitle: Back to the Gold Saucer — the date and the keystone

video.creator: Shirrako
video.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth — Full Gameplay Walkthrough (4K, No Commentary)

objectives.label: Return to the Gold Saucer for the keystone
Experience the famous evening date event
Set up the journey to the Temple of the Ancients

steps.text: Return to the Gold Saucer in pursuit of the keystone needed for the Temple of the Ancients.
The chapter contains the celebrated evening date event. Your dialogue and side-quest choices across the game decide which companion shares the gondola with you.
With the keystone in hand, the party sets course for the Temple of the Ancients.

sections.title: Where Angels Fear to Tread
sections.subtitle: The Temple of the Ancients — the shifting dungeon

video.creator: Shirrako
video.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth — Full Gameplay Walkthrough (4K, No Commentary)

objectives.label: Navigate the shifting Temple of the Ancients
Solve the temple's spatial puzzles
Survive the temple's boss gauntlet

steps.text: Enter the Temple of the Ancients, a shape-shifting dungeon of spatial puzzles. Take it room by room and watch how the geometry changes around you.
The temple stacks several boss encounters back to back — manage MP and ATB carefully and lean on staggers.
The temple's events set the stage for the journey's most painful turn at the Forgotten Capital. Steel yourself — and stock revives and healing — before the final chapter.

video.creator: Shirrako
video.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth — Full Gameplay Walkthrough (4K, No Commentary)

objectives.label: Reach the Forgotten Capital
Survive the climactic multi-phase battles
See the journey's end — and its cliffhanger

steps.text: Reach the Forgotten Capital. This chapter is almost entirely story and boss battles — come fully stocked with Phoenix Downs, Hi-Potions, and a tuned materia loadout.
Fight through the multi-phase finale. The closing battles escalate through several forms and party splits; keep at least one reviver and one healer per group.
Rebirth ends on a deliberately ambiguous, emotional note that sets up the trilogy's final part. Save a clear-data file for the post-game and hard-mode content.

locations.name: Grasslands
locations.reachableBy: The first open region, around Kalm and the Chocobo Ranch — your introduction to Chadley and World Intel.

locations.name: Junon
locations.reachableBy: Coastal beaches and marshes leading to Under Junon and the cannon-city, with the parade set-piece.

locations.name: Corel
locations.reachableBy: The largest span — desert and mountains housing Costa del Sol and the Gold Saucer.

locations.name: Gongaga
locations.reachableBy: Dense jungle with mushroom-springboard traversal and Zack/reactor story beats.

locations.name: Cosmo Canyon
locations.reachableBy: Red XIII's homeland — canyon terrain tied to planet lore and the Gi.

locations.name: Nibel
locations.reachableBy: The mountains around Nibelheim, central to the Sephiroth flashback and Mt. Nibel.

locations.name: Meridian Ocean
locations.reachableBy: Open-ocean traversal that connects the regions once the world fully opens.

locations.name: Costa del Sol (hub)
locations.reachableBy: Seaside resort in Corel with beach minigames and downtime.

locations.name: Gold Saucer (hub)
locations.reachableBy: The amusement-park hub in Corel — the densest cluster of minigames in the game.
